summary: An advance care planning model is feasible in community palliative care due to its integration into existing interdisciplinary programs such as PACE, support from regulations like the Patient Self-Determination Act, and consistent positive outcomes—including improved quality of life, lower costs, and enhanced patient-family satisfaction—in routine outpatient settings. These findings demonstrate practical applicability and sustainability without contradicting observed challenges related to access disparities.
